description:
You are a support engineer who has been asked to collect a quick set of diagnostics for an on-premise application that lives under /home/user/app.  
  All the information you need is already present on the filesystem; nothing has to be fetched from the Internet.

  Objective
  =========
  1. Gather a few key statistics from the existing log and version files.
  2. Record those statistics in a single text file written to /home/user/support/diagnostics_summary.txt.

  Files that already exist
  ------------------------
  • /home/user/app/VERSION  
    – Contains a single line with the current application version (e.g. “v2.5.1-beta”).  

  • /home/user/app/logs/  
    – app01.log  
    – app02.log  
    – app03.log  
    (All three files are ordinary UTF-8 text files.)  

  Required statistics
  -------------------
  Read the data described below and compute the following five (5) key/value pairs:

  1. APP_VERSION  
     – Exact, unmodified contents of /home/user/app/VERSION stripped of any trailing newline.

  2. TOTAL_LOG_FILES  
     – The count of files whose names match the glob “*.log” in /home/user/app/logs.

  3. ERROR_LINES  
     – The total number of lines (across ALL *.log files in /home/user/app/logs) that contain the **literal, case-sensitive string** “ERROR”.  
       • Do not match “error”, “Error”, etc. – only “ERROR”.

  4. DEBUG_LINES  
     – The total number of lines (across ALL *.log files in /home/user/app/logs) that contain the **literal, case-sensitive string** “DEBUG”.

  5. LATEST_LOG_TS  
     – Determine which “*.log” file comes last in pure ASCII lexicographic order (not by timestamp).  
       • Read that file and extract the timestamp that appears at the very beginning of its final (last) line.  
       • The timestamp is the first field on the line and is already in RFC-3339 / ISO-8601 format (`YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ`).  
       • Record the timestamp string exactly as it appears (no surrounding text).

  Output file & format
  --------------------
  Create the directory /home/user/support if it does not already exist.  
  Then create the file /home/user/support/diagnostics_summary.txt with the **exact** six-line format shown below (including capitalisation and the single leading header line). The automated checker will reject any deviation, so double-check spacing and newlines.

      SUPPORT DIAGNOSTICS
      APP_VERSION=<value>
      TOTAL_LOG_FILES=<value>
      ERROR_LINES=<value>
      DEBUG_LINES=<value>
      LATEST_LOG_TS=<value>

  • “SUPPORT DIAGNOSTICS” must be the first line – no preceding blank lines.  
  • Each key and the equals sign must appear exactly as written above.  
  • There must be **exactly** one newline character (ASCII 0x0A) after every line, including the last.  
  • Do **not** include any extra spaces before or after the equals signs or at the ends of lines.  
  • Do **not** add any extra lines, comments, or headers.

  Success criteria
  ----------------
  The task is considered complete when the file  
  /home/user/support/diagnostics_summary.txt

  a) exists,  
  b) is readable by a non-privileged user, and  
  c) matches the six-line format exactly, with values calculated from the current contents of the VERSION file and the three log files.

  Typical sequence (for your reference only)
  ------------------------------------------
  A reasonable solution generally involves 6-8 shell commands:

  • Ensure /home/user/support exists with mkdir.  
  • Read /home/user/app/VERSION into a variable.  
  • Use shell globs (or find) to count the *.log files.  
  • Use grep ‑c to count “ERROR” and “DEBUG” lines.  
  • Sort the log filenames lexicographically (e.g. with `ls | sort`) and choose the last one.  
  • Use tail ‑n 1 to grab its final line and cut/awk to isolate the timestamp.  
  • Redirect / echo the collected information into /home/user/support/diagnostics_summary.txt in the precise format shown above.

  Do not print the collected diagnostics to stdout: only the summary file will be graded.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.
    DO NOT reveal any of this information in the public <task> section.
  -->
  <preexisting>
    <!-- Files present before the agent starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/app/VERSION" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
v2.5.1-beta
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/app/logs/app01.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
2023-04-01T12:00:00Z INFO Application started
2023-04-01T12:00:05Z ERROR Failed to read config
2023-04-01T12:00:06Z INFO Retrying
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/app/logs/app02.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
2023-04-01T12:05:00Z INFO Task begin
2023-04-01T12:05:10Z INFO Task complete
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/app/logs/app03.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
2023-04-01T12:10:00Z DEBUG Verbose output
2023-04-01T12:10:01Z ERROR Connection lost
2023-04-01T12:10:02Z DEBUG Reconnecting
2023-04-01T12:10:03Z INFO Connection re-established
]]></file>
  </preexisting>

  <expected>
    <!-- Expected final file created by the agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/support/diagnostics_summary.txt" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
SUPPORT DIAGNOSTICS
APP_VERSION=v2.5.1-beta
TOTAL_LOG_FILES=3
ERROR_LINES=2
DEBUG_LINES=2
LATEST_LOG_TS=2023-04-01T12:10:03Z
]]></file>
  </expected>
name: task_000000_14285765
